Unit tests for the `link` module in the `pebble-firmware` codebase, testing interface and socket functionalities.

# Purpose
The code is a test suite for a network communication interface, specifically testing the functionality of a `link.Interface` and `link.InterfaceSocket` from the `pebble.pulse2` module. It uses the `unittest` framework to define a series of test cases that verify the behavior of these components under various conditions. The `FakeIOStream` class simulates an I/O stream for testing purposes, allowing the tests to run without actual network communication. The tests cover scenarios such as sending and receiving data, handling socket connections, and managing the lifecycle of the interface and sockets, including opening, closing, and error handling.

The test suite is organized into three main classes: `TestInterface`, `TestInterfaceSocket`, and `TestLink`. Each class focuses on a specific aspect of the network interface. `TestInterface` tests the `link.Interface` class, ensuring it can send packets, manage connections, and handle link control protocol (LCP) events. `TestInterfaceSocket` tests the `link.InterfaceSocket` class, verifying socket operations such as sending data, closing sockets, and handling packets. `TestLink` tests the `link.Link` class, focusing on opening and closing sockets and managing transport layers. The tests use mock objects to simulate dependencies and isolate the components under test.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `__future__.absolute_import`
- `threading`
- `time`
- `unittest`
- `unittest.mock`
- `mock`
- `queue`
- `Queue`
- `pebble.pulse2.exceptions`
- `pebble.pulse2.framing`
- `pebble.pulse2.link`
- `pebble.pulse2.ppp`

- **Description**: Simulates an I/O stream with separate read and write queues, allowing for controlled data flow in testing environments. It provides methods to read from and write to the queues, and to close the stream, which prevents further operations. The class also includes a method to retrieve all data written to the stream.

#### FakeIOStream\.read<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.FakeIOStream.read}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L41>)

Reads data from the `read_queue` if the stream is open, otherwise raises an error.
- **Inputs**:
    - `length`: The number of bytes to read from the stream (not used in the method logic).
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the `closed` attribute is `True`, and if so, raises an `IOError` indicating that the operation is on a closed stream.
    - Attempts to retrieve an item from the `read_queue` with a timeout of 0.001 seconds.
    - If the `read_queue` is empty, catches the `queue.Empty` exception and returns an empty byte string `b''`.
- **Output**: Returns the data retrieved from the `read_queue` or an empty byte string if the queue is empty.
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.FakeIOStream`](<#FakeIOStream>)  (Base Class)

- **Inputs**:
    - `data`: The data to write to the write queue.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Checks if the stream is closed by evaluating the `closed` attribute.
    - Raises an `IOError` if the stream is closed.
    - Puts the `data` into the `write_queue` if the stream is open.
- **Output**: No return value.

#### FakeIOStream\.pop\_all\_written\_data<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.FakeIOStream.pop_all_written_data}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L57>)

Retrieves and removes all items from the `write_queue` of the `FakeIOStream` instance.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Initialize an empty list `data` to store items from the `write_queue`.
    - Enter a `try` block to attempt to retrieve items from the `write_queue`.
    - Use a `while True` loop to continuously attempt to get items from the `write_queue` without waiting.
    - Append each item retrieved from the `write_queue` to the `data` list.
    - Catch the `queue.Empty` exception when the `write_queue` is empty, and exit the loop.
    - Return the `data` list containing all items that were in the `write_queue`.
- **Output**: A list of all items that were in the `write_queue` before it was emptied.
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.FakeIOStream`](<#FakeIOStream>)  (Base Class)

#### TestInterface\.setUp<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.setUp}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L69>)

Initializes test environment by setting up a fake IO stream and configuring the unit under test (UUT) with specific parameters.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest.TestCase`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Creates an instance of [`FakeIOStream`](<#FakeIOStream>) and assigns it to `self.iostream`.
    - Initializes the unit under test (`uut`) as an instance of `link.Interface` using `self.iostream`.
    - Registers `self.iostream.close` as a cleanup function to be called after each test.
    - Overrides the LCP restart timeout of `self.uut` to `0.001` to speed up test execution.
    - Replaces the LCP ping method of `self.uut` with `self.fake_ping`.
    - Sets `self.ping_should_succeed` to `True` to control the behavior of the fake ping method.
- **Output**: No output is returned; it sets up the test environment for subsequent test methods.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.FakeIOStream`](<#FakeIOStream>)

Tests if the [`send_packet`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfacesend_packet>) method of the `Interface` class correctly sends a packet and writes the expected data to the `FakeIOStream`.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest.expectedFailure`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`send_packet`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfacesend_packet>) method on the `uut` (unit under test) with a protocol identifier `0x8889` and data `b'data'`.
    - Encapsulates the data using `ppp.encapsulate` and encodes it with `framing.encode_frame`.
    - Retrieves all written data from the `iostream` using [`pop_all_written_data`](<#FakeIOStreampop_all_written_data>).
    - Asserts that the encoded frame is present in the retrieved data from the `iostream`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to validate behavior.

#### TestInterface\.test\_interface\_closing\_closes\_sockets\_and\_iostream<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.test_interface_closing_closes_sockets_and_iostream}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L95>)

Verifies that closing the interface also closes all connected sockets and the associated IO stream.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Connects to two sockets using `self.uut.connect` with protocol identifiers `0xf0f1` and `0xf0f3`.
    - Calls `self.uut.close()` to close the interface.
    - Asserts that `socket1` is closed using `self.assertTrue(socket1.closed)`.
    - Asserts that `socket2` is closed using `self.assertTrue(socket2.closed)`.
    - Asserts that `self.iostream` is closed using `self.assertTrue(self.iostream.closed)`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to verify behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.connect`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceconnect>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.close`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceclose>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface`](<#TestInterface>)  (Base Class)

#### TestInterface\.test\_iostream\_closing\_closes\_interface\_and\_sockets<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.test_iostream_closing_closes_interface_and_sockets}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L103>)

Tests if closing the `iostream` also closes the `Interface` and its associated socket.
- **Inputs**:
    - `self`: Instance of the `TestInterface` class.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Connects to a socket using `self.uut.connect(0xf0f1)` and assigns it to `socket`.
    - Closes the `iostream` using `self.iostream.close()`.
    - Pauses execution for 0.01 seconds to allow the receive thread to detect the closure.
    - Asserts that the `Interface` (`self.uut`) is closed using `self.assertTrue(self.uut.closed)`.
    - Asserts that the `socket` is closed using `self.assertTrue(socket.closed)`.
- **Output**: No return value; uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.connect`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceconnect>)

Tests that the [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) method returns `None` when the Link Control Protocol (LCP) is down.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest.expectedFailure`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) method on the `uut` (unit under test) with a `timeout` of 0.
    - Asserts that the result of [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) is `None`, indicating that the LCP is down.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the test asserts that [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) returns `None`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>)

- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:

    - Uses `assertRaisesRegex` to verify that calling [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) on the closed interface raises a `ValueError` with the message 'closed interface'.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the test passes if the expected exception is raised.

#### TestInterface\.test\_get\_link\_when\_lcp\_is\_up<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.test_get_link_when_lcp_is_up}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L134>)

Tests if the [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) method returns a non-None value when the link control protocol (LCP) is up.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest.expectedFailure`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`on_link_up`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_up>) method on the unit under test (`uut`) to simulate the link being up.
    - Calls the [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) method on `uut` with a `timeout` of 0.
    - Asserts that the result of [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) is not `None`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.on_link_up`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_up>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface`](<#TestInterface>)  (Base Class)

#### TestInterface\.test\_link\_object\_is\_closed\_when\_lcp\_goes\_down<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.test_link_object_is_closed_when_lcp_goes_down}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L138>)

Tests that a link object is closed when the Link Control Protocol (LCP) goes down.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `self.uut.on_link_up()` to simulate the link going up.
    - Retrieves the link object using `self.uut.get_link(timeout=0)` and checks that it is not closed with `self.assertFalse(link.closed)`.
    - Calls `self.uut.on_link_down()` to simulate the link going down.
    - Asserts that the link object is closed with `self.assertTrue(link.closed)`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.on_link_up`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_up>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.on_link_down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_down>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface`](<#TestInterface>)  (Base Class)

#### TestInterface\.test\_lcp\_bouncing\_doesnt\_reopen\_old\_link\_object<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.test_lcp_bouncing_doesnt_reopen_old_link_object}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L145>)

Tests that a new link object is created when the link control protocol (LCP) bounces, ensuring the old link object remains closed.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest.expectedFailure`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `self.uut.on_link_up()` to simulate the link going up.
    - Retrieves the first link object `link1` using `self.uut.get_link(timeout=0)`.
    - Calls `self.uut.on_link_down()` to simulate the link going down.
    - Calls `self.uut.on_link_up()` again to simulate the link going up a second time.
    - Retrieves the second link object `link2` using `self.uut.get_link(timeout=0)`.
    - Asserts that `link1` is closed using `self.assertTrue(link1.closed)`.
    - Asserts that `link2` is not closed using `self.assertFalse(link2.closed)`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.on_link_up`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_up>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.on_link_down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_down>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface`](<#TestInterface>)  (Base Class)


---
#### TestInterface\.test\_close\_gracefully\_shuts\_down\_lcp<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.test_close_gracefully_shuts_down_lcp}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L154>)

Verifies that the [`close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>) method of the `uut` object correctly shuts down the LCP (Link Control Protocol) and sets the `is_finished` event.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest.expectedFailure`
- **Inputs**:
    - `self`: Represents the instance of the `TestInterface` class.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls `receive_configure_request_acceptable` on the `lcp` object of `uut` with parameters `0` and an empty byte string.
    - Calls `receive_configure_ack` on the `lcp` object of `uut`.
    - Calls the [`close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>) method on the `uut` object.
    - Asserts that the `is_finished` event of the `lcp` object is set.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method asserts the state of the `is_finished` event.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/pcmp.PulseControlMessageProtocol.close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface`](<#TestInterface>)  (Base Class)

#### TestInterface\.test\_ping\_failure\_triggers\_lcp\_restart<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface.test_ping_failure_triggers_lcp_restart}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L160>)

Tests that a ping failure triggers a restart of the LCP (Link Control Protocol).
- **Decorators**: `@mock`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets `ping_should_succeed` to `False` to simulate a ping failure.
    - Mocks the `restart` method of the `lcp` object to track its invocation.
    - Calls [`on_link_up`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_up>) to simulate the link being up.
    - Asserts that [`get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>) returns `None`, indicating the link is down due to the ping failure.
    - Verifies that the `restart` method of the `lcp` object is called exactly once.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.on_link_up`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceon_link_up>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Interface.get_link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Interfaceget_link>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterface`](<#TestInterface>)  (Base Class)

### TestInterfaceSocket<!-- {{#class:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L168>)

- **Description**: Implements unit tests for the `InterfaceSocket` class, ensuring correct behavior for socket operations such as sending data, closing the socket, and handling packets. Tests include verifying that the socket is not closed upon construction, that sending data calls the appropriate interface method, and that closing the socket sets it as closed and unregisters it from the interface. Additional tests check for error handling when sending data after the socket is closed, and ensure that the context manager functionality and idempotency of the close operation are correct.
- **Methods**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.setUp`](<#TestInterfaceSocketsetUp>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_socket_is_not_closed_when_constructed`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_socket_is_not_closed_when_constructed>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_send`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_send>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_close_sets_socket_as_closed`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_close_sets_socket_as_closed>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_close_unregisters_socket_with_interface`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_close_unregisters_socket_with_interface>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_close_calls_on_close_handler`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_close_calls_on_close_handler>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_send_after_close_is_an_error`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_send_after_close_is_an_error>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_handle_packet`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_handle_packet>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_handle_packet_does_not_call_on_packet_handler_after_close`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_handle_packet_does_not_call_on_packet_handler_after_close>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_context_manager`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_context_manager>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_close_is_idempotent`](<#TestInterfaceSockettest_close_is_idempotent>)
- **Inherits From**:
    - `unittest.TestCase`

**Methods**

Initializes the test environment by creating a mock interface and an [`InterfaceSocket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#InterfaceSocket>) instance.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Creates a mock object `self.interface` using `mock.MagicMock()` to simulate the interface behavior.
    - Initializes `self.uut` as an instance of `link.InterfaceSocket` with `self.interface` and a protocol identifier `0xf2f1`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method sets up the test environment for subsequent test methods.

- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`send`](<../pebble/pulse2/transports.py.md#Socketsend>) method on the `uut` (unit under test) with the byte data `b'data'`.
    - Asserts that the `send_packet` method of the `interface` mock is called exactly once with the arguments `0xf2f1` and `b'data'`.

Verifies that calling the [`close`](<#FakeIOStreamclose>) method on a socket sets its `closed` attribute to `True`.

- **Inputs**:
    - `self`: Represents the instance of the `TestInterfaceSocket` class.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call the [`close`](<#FakeIOStreamclose>) method on the `uut` object, which is an instance of `link.InterfaceSocket`.
    - Use `assertTrue` to check if the `closed` attribute of `uut` is `True`.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method asserts the state of the `closed` attribute.

Verifies that the `on_close` handler is called exactly once when the [`close`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#InterfaceSocketclose>) method is invoked on the `uut` object.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Create a mock object `on_close`.
    - Assign the mock object `on_close` to the `on_close` attribute of `self.uut`.
    - Call the [`close`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#InterfaceSocketclose>) method on `self.uut`.
    - Assert that the `on_close` mock object was called exactly once with no arguments.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method asserts the behavior of the `on_close` handler.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.InterfaceSocket.close`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#InterfaceSocketclose>)

#### TestInterfaceSocket\.test\_handle\_packet\_does\_not\_call\_on\_packet\_handler\_after\_close<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_handle_packet_does_not_call_on_packet_handler_after_close}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L205>)

Verifies that the `on_packet` handler is not called after the socket is closed.
- **Inputs**:
    - `self`: Represents the instance of the `TestInterfaceSocket` class.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Create a mock object `on_packet`.
    - Assign the mock object `on_packet` to `self.uut.on_packet`.
    - Call the [`close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>) method on `self.uut` to close the socket.
    - Call the [`handle_packet`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#InterfaceSockethandle_packet>) method on `self.uut` with the byte data `b'data'`.
    - Assert that the `on_packet` mock object was not called.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the test asserts that `on_packet` is not called.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/pcmp.PulseControlMessageProtocol.close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>)

#### TestInterfaceSocket\.test\_context\_manager<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_context_manager}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L212>)

Tests the context manager behavior of the `uut` object, ensuring it is closed after exiting the context.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Enter the context manager with `self.uut` as `uut`.
    - Assert that `uut` is the same as `self.uut`.
    - Assert that `self.uut.closed` is `False` inside the context.
    - Exit the context manager, which should close `self.uut`.
    - Assert that `self.uut.closed` is `True` after exiting the context.
- **Output**: No output is returned; assertions validate the context manager behavior.
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket`](<#TestInterfaceSocket>)  (Base Class)


---
#### TestInterfaceSocket\.test\_close\_is\_idempotent<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket.test_close_is_idempotent}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L218>)

Verifies that calling the [`close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>) method on a socket multiple times does not result in multiple unregistrations or multiple calls to the `on_close` handler.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Create a mock object `on_close` to simulate the `on_close` handler.
    - Assign the mock `on_close` to the `on_close` attribute of `self.uut`.
    - Call the [`close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>) method on `self.uut` twice.
    - Assert that the `unregister_socket` method of `self.interface` is called exactly once.
    - Assert that the `on_close` mock is called exactly once.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to verify behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/pcmp.PulseControlMessageProtocol.close`](<../pebble/pulse2/pcmp.py.md#PulseControlMessageProtocolclose>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestInterfaceSocket`](<#TestInterfaceSocket>)  (Base Class)

### TestLink<!-- {{#class:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L227>)

- **Description**: Tests the `link.Link` class by simulating various scenarios such as opening sockets, handling transport errors, and ensuring proper closure of links. It uses mock objects to replace actual transport mechanisms and verifies the behavior of the `link.Link` class under different conditions.
- **Methods**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.setUp`](<#TestLinksetUp>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_open_socket`](<#TestLinktest_open_socket>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_down`](<#TestLinktest_down>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_on_close_callback_when_going_down`](<#TestLinktest_on_close_callback_when_going_down>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_open_socket_after_down_is_an_error`](<#TestLinktest_open_socket_after_down_is_an_error>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_open_socket_with_bad_transport_name`](<#TestLinktest_open_socket_with_bad_transport_name>)
- **Inherits From**:
    - `unittest.TestCase`

**Methods**

#### TestLink\.setUp<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.setUp}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L229>)

Sets up the test environment by patching the `TRANSPORTS` dictionary and initializing the [`Link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Link>) object under test.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Patches the `TRANSPORTS` dictionary in the `link.Link` class with a mock transport named 'fake'.
    - Starts the patcher to apply the changes to the `TRANSPORTS` dictionary.
    - Registers a cleanup function to stop the patcher after the test completes.
    - Initializes the `uut` (unit under test) as an instance of `link.Link` with a mock object and a maximum transmission unit (MTU) size of 1500.
- **Output**: No output is returned; it sets up the test environment for subsequent test methods.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Link`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Link>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink`](<#TestLink>)  (Base Class)

#### TestLink\.test\_open\_socket<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_open_socket}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L237>)

Tests the [`open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkopen_socket>) method of the `Link` class to ensure it opens a socket with the specified transport and verifies the call.
- **Decorators**: `@unittest.expectedFailure`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkopen_socket>) method on the `uut` (unit under test) with transport set to 'fake', port set to `0xabcd`, and timeout set to `1.0`.
    - Asserts that the [`open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkopen_socket>) method of the 'fake' transport is called once with the specified port and timeout.
    - Checks that the returned `socket` is the same as the one returned by the [`open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkopen_socket>) method of the 'fake' transport.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Link.open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkopen_socket>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink`](<#TestLink>)  (Base Class)

#### TestLink\.test\_down<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_down}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L244>)

Tests the [`down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>) method of the `uut` object to ensure it closes the link and calls the [`down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>) method on the 'fake' transport.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>) method on the `uut` object.
    - Asserts that the `uut` object is closed by checking the `closed` attribute.
    - Verifies that the [`down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>) method on the 'fake' transport is called exactly once.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method uses assertions to validate behavior.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Link.down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink`](<#TestLink>)  (Base Class)

#### TestLink\.test\_on\_close\_callback\_when\_going\_down<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_on_close_callback_when_going_down}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L249>)

Tests if the `on_close` callback is called when the `Link` object goes down.
- **Decorators**: `@mock.patch.dict`
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets `self.uut.on_close` to a mock object to track calls to the `on_close` callback.
    - Calls the [`down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>) method on `self.uut` to simulate the `Link` object going down.
    - Asserts that the `on_close` callback is called exactly once with no arguments.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the method asserts the behavior of the `on_close` callback.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Link.down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink`](<#TestLink>)  (Base Class)

#### TestLink\.test\_open\_socket\_after\_down\_is\_an\_error<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_open_socket_after_down_is_an_error}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L254>)

Tests that opening a socket after the link is down raises a ValueError.
- **Inputs**:
    - `self`: Represents the instance of the class `TestLink`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>) method on `self.uut` to set the link to a down state.
    - Uses `assertRaisesRegex` to verify that calling [`open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/transports.py.md#BestEffortTransportBaseopen_socket>) on `self.uut` with arguments `'fake'` and `0xabcd` raises a `ValueError` with the message 'closed Link'.
- **Output**: No output is returned; the test passes if the expected exception is raised.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Link.down`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkdown>)
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/transports.BestEffortTransportBase.open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/transports.py.md#BestEffortTransportBaseopen_socket>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink`](<#TestLink>)  (Base Class)

#### TestLink\.test\_open\_socket\_with\_bad\_transport\_name<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink.test_open_socket_with_bad_transport_name}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.py#L259>)

Tests that opening a socket with an invalid transport name raises a KeyError.
- **Inputs**:
    - `self`: Represents the instance of the class `TestLink`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Uses `assertRaisesRegex` to check if a `KeyError` is raised when calling [`open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkopen_socket>) with a transport name 'bad'.
    - The error message must match the regex pattern "Unknown transport 'bad'".
- **Output**: No output is returned; the test passes if the expected exception is raised.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/pebble/pulse2/link.Link.open_socket`](<../pebble/pulse2/link.py.md#Linkopen_socket>)
- **See also**: [`pebble-firmware/python_libs/pulse2/tests/test_link.TestLink`](<#TestLink>)  (Base Class)
